source.title: directed evolution of a cyclodipeptide synthase with new activities via label fr

initial_request.research_problem: Directed evolution of enzymes is limited by the lack of high-throughput screening (HTS) assays for new catalytic activities. Mass spectrometry (MS) is label-free and suitable for untargeted discovery but traditionally slow. The goal is to develop an automated, label-free MS screening workflow for directed evolution of new enzymatic activities, demonstrated by engineering a cyclodipeptide synthase (CDPS) to produce new diketopiperazine (DKP) products.
initial_request.known_context: CDPSs are recalcitrant targets for rational engineering, and large-scale screening of CDPS variants has not been reported due to lack of HTS assays.
constraints: High-throughput screening assay must be label-free and automated.
Must detect new DKP products not produced by wild-type AlbC.
input_data: List of 190 possible DKP molecules with theoretical m/z values (115–373 Da)
quantifiable_goals: Screen ~4500 epPCR clones within a week.
Identify at least one AlbC mutant producing a new DKP product.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No high-throughput screening assay exists for new catalytic activities of CDPSs.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Lack of HTS assays limits directed evolution of CDPSs for new DKP products.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: An automated, label-free MS screening workflow can enable high-throughput directed evolution of CDPSs to produce new DKP products.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop an automated workflow integrating library creation, robotic cultivation, MALDI-ToF MS, and custom computational analysis.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Assay development for high-throughput mutant screening remains challenging, particularly for new catalytic activities. CDPSs are recalcitrant targets for rational engineering, and large-scale screening of CDPS variants has not been reported due to lack of HTS assays.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design an automated workflow to address the screening bottleneck.

evidence.quote_or_span: assay development for high-throughput mutant screening remains challenging, particularly for new catalytic activities.
CDPSs are recognized as recalcitrant targets for rational engineering.
large-scale screening of CDPS variants has not been reported, possibly due to the lack of applicable HTS assays.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No automated MS screening workflow exists for CDPS directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to design a workflow that can create libraries, cultivate mutants, and perform label-free MS screening at high throughput.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: An integrated robotic workcell can automate library creation, cultivation, and MALDI-ToF MS analysis at 5 s per sample, enabling screening of thousands of clones.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design workflow with SSM and epPCR libraries, robotic cultivation, and MALDI-ToF MS analysis; choose AlbC as proof-of-concept target.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Workflow designed to automatically create and cultivate SSM and epPCR libraries, robotically process culture supernatants for MALDI-ToF MS at 5 s per sample, and analyze data with custom algorithms.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Implement the workflow and validate with wild-type AlbC.

evidence.quote_or_span: Here we report an automated workflow for directed evolution of new enzymatic activities via high-throughput library creation and label-free MS screening.
For a proof of concept, we chose to engineer a cyclodipeptide synthase (CDPS) that synthesizes diketopiperazine (DKP) compounds with therapeutic potential.
site-saturation mutagenesis (SSM) and error-prone PCR (epPCR) libraries expressing CDPS mutants were automatically created and cultivated on an integrated work cell.
Culture supernatants were then robotically processed for matrix-assisted laser desorption/ionization time-of-flight (MALDI-ToF) MS analysis at a rate of 5 s per sample.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Workflow designed but not yet validated with actual samples.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm that the automated MS workflow can detect known CDP products from wild-type AlbC.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The automated workflow will detect the expected CDP products from WT AlbC-expressing E. coli, consistent with literature.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Run WT AlbC-expressing E. coli through the automated workflow and analyze MS data.

parameters.sample: WT AlbC-expressing E. coli culture supernatant

evolution_trajectory.observation: Known CDP products were detected, validating the workflow.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to screen mutant libraries for new products.

evidence.quote_or_span: These results validated our workflow to detect CDP products from microplate cultures of AlbC-expressing E. coli using MALDI-ToF MS.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Workflow validated with WT, but no new products identified yet.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: SSM libraries targeting binding pocket residues may yield new DKP products.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutating binding pocket residues via SSM will alter substrate specificity and produce new DKP products.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Create and screen 14 SSM libraries targeting binding pocket residues and some external residues.
evolution_trajectory.observation: No new CDP molecules were observed from the 14 SSM library mutants.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: SSM limited to predefined residues failed; need random mutagenesis across the whole protein.

evidence.quote_or_span: Unfortunately, we did not observe any new CDP molecules produced from the 14 SSM library mutants.
Unfortunately, we did not observe any new CDP molecules produced from the 14 SSM library mutants.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: SSM libraries failed to produce new products.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Random mutations throughout the whole protein may uncover new specificity determinants not predictable by rational design.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: epPCR libraries with random mutations will generate variants with altered substrate specificity, including new DKP products.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design epPCR libraries with an average of 2 nucleotide changes per gene to introduce random mutations across the entire AlbC sequence.

tool_or_method.name: Error-prone PCR (epPCR) library design
tool_or_method.version: Agilent GeneMorph II Random Mutagenesis Kit

parameters.mutation_rate: on average 2 nucleotides per gene variant

evolution_trajectory.observation: epPCR libraries created with random mutations throughout the whole protein sequence.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Screen the epPCR libraries using the automated MS workflow.

evidence.quote_or_span: Therefore, we further turned to profile epPCR libraries that contain random mutations throughout the whole protein sequence.

Under optimized conditions using the Agilent GeneMorph II Random Mutagenesis Kit, on average 2 nucleotides were introduced per gene variant so that most epPCR library members contain no more than one amino acid change.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: epPCR libraries designed but not yet screened.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to screen a large number of epPCR clones to find rare variants with new activities.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Screening ~4500 epPCR clones will identify mutants producing new DKP products.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen around 4500 independent epPCR clones using the automated MS workflow.

parameters.screening_method: MALDI-ToF MS with custom computational analysis

evolution_trajectory.observation: In total around 4500 independent clones were screened using the above workflow.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze MS data to identify clones with new peaks.

evidence.quote_or_span: In total around 4500 independent clones were screened using the above workflow.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MS data collected from ~4500 epPCR clones.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to statistically identify new m/z peaks corresponding to new DKP products.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: One-way ANOVA with Tukey's multiple comparison test will reveal clones with significantly different peak intensities at theoretical m/z values of possible DKP molecules.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform multivariant analysis of 108 theoretical m/z values of 190 possible DKP molecules (mass window 115–373 Da) using one-way ANOVA with Tukey's test.

tool_or_method.name: Custom computational algorithms for MS data analysis

parameters.statistical_test: one-way ANOVA with Tukey's multiple comparison test

evolution_trajectory.observation: A new peak corresponding to a new cyclopeptide product was identified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Isolate and validate the mutant producing the new peak.

evidence.quote_or_span: The resulting mass spectral data were processed via custom computational algorithms, which performed a multivariant analysis of 108 theoretical mass-to-charge (m/z) values of 190 possible DKP molecules within a mass window of 115–373 Da.
The peak intensities at the theoretical m/z values of predicted cyclodipeptide ions in each MALDI mass spectrum were analyzed by the one-way ANOVA with Tukey's multiple comparison test.

Notably, by creating and screening $\sim$ 4500 epPCR library members within a week using unlabeled MS on an integrated, robotic workcell, an AlbC mutant producing a new cyclopeptide product was identified, revealing a previously unknown residue (F186) that exerts a substantial impact on substrate specificity.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: A new peak detected in some epPCR clones.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm the identity of the mutant and the new product.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The clone producing the new peak harbors a specific mutation (F186L) that enables synthesis of cyclo(L-Phe-L-Val) (cFV).
evolution_trajectory.decision: Isolate the clone, perform Sanger sequencing to identify the mutation, and confirm cFV production by LC-MS/MS.
evolution_trajectory.observation: An F186L CDPS mutant was isolated to produce cyclo(L-Phe-L-Val) (cFV), undetectable in wild-type. Sanger sequencing confirmed the F186L mutation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Investigate the structural mechanism of the F186L mutation.

evidence.quote_or_span: An F186L CDPS mutant was isolated to produce cyclo(L-Phe-L-Val), which is undetectable in the product profile of the wild-type enzyme.
4A), all of which harbored the F186L mutation as revealed by Sanger sequencing results.
(B) LC-MS traces of WT and F186L mutant.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: F186L mutant validated to produce cFV.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: The structural basis for the altered substrate specificity is unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The F186L mutation increases the binding cavity volume and alters substrate binding energy, favoring cFV production.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform computational modeling (docking and MD simulations) of WT and F186L with cFL and cFV substrates.
evolution_trajectory.observation: F186L mutation increases the binding cavity volume and significantly increases binding energy when docking with cFV.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude the study and note limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: The results showed that the binding energy of F186L was significantly increased when docking with the substrate cFV (Table 2).
Moreover, cFL and the new derivative cFV were separately docked into the binding pockets of the WT and F186L variant for 100 ns MD simulations (Fig.
Then, we investigated possible mechanisms underlying new substrate specificity of the F186L variant from structural aspects using computational modeling.
Overall, it is possible that an increase in the production of cFV occurs precisely due to the larger volume of the substrate-binding cavity in F186L.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: The study has limitations: only one new-activity mutant reported, and untargeted MS screening requires careful optimization.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The workflow is effective but may miss weakly active variants; further optimization could improve sensitivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitations and suggest future improvements.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Only one mutant (F186L) reported with new activity; other variants not detailed; untargeted MS screening requires careful optimization to detect weak signals.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Study completed; limitations noted for future work.

evidence.quote_or_span: Notably, by creating and screening $\sim$ 4500 epPCR library members within a week using unlabeled MS on an integrated, robotic workcell, an AlbC mutant producing a new cyclopeptide product was identified, revealing a previously unknown residue (F186) that exerts a substantial impact on substrate specificity.

When comparing LC-MS/MS traces, we also noticed substantial reduction of the native main product cFL in the F186L variant relative to WT, and a slight enhancement in production of other products including cFM, cFF, and cFY, indicating a shift towards more bulky substrates with the F186L mutant (Fig.

success_verification.final_conclusion: An automated, label-free MS screening workflow successfully identified an F186L AlbC mutant producing a new DKP product (cFV), demonstrating the feasibility of high-throughput directed evolution for new enzymatic activities.
limitations: Only one mutant (F186L) reported with new activity; other variants not detailed.
Untargeted MS screening requires careful optimization to detect weak signals.
